reviews.review_text:
Cons: The map is only 1/3 of the size of the map from the first game, there aren't many leviathans, the Cyclops is absent, and the base swimming speed has been decreased.

Pros: Extremely polished, has added tons of quality-of-life improvements, huge upgrades to base building, new biomes to explore and creatures to see (and/or eat), more land exploration (could be a con to some, it is subjective), the Seatruck is very fun to use, it adds a hoverbike for land travel, and adds lots of new gadgetry both alien and Alterra. I don't think the story was quite as good as it was in the first game but it was interesting and I liked piecing together the mystery of it all via the audio logs found in numerous places.

My take: When it came out, many people went into it expecting a Subnautica sequel, but this is not Subnautica 2, this is a spinoff game for those who have already completed the first one. I think that for those who really enjoyed the first Subnautica this game is worth playing and exploring because personally I had a lot of fun on both of my playthroughs.

So I recently Subnautica (over like a 4 year period lol) and I wanted to play sub-zero before Subnautica 2 comes out and I wasn't sure what to expect because of some of the negativity involved with this game. Honestly I really enjoyed this game compared to the first one and I think the sea truck actually fits this game pretty good.

Personally I love the enclosed cave sections and a larger vehicle like a cyclops wouldn't comfortably fit so I can understand why it wasn't included in this game. I'm not saying the upgraded sea truck is a better replacement for the cyclops but I think it works really well in this game and I enjoyed experimenting with the different modules.

This game also focuses much more on Lore and your purpose on the planet which I enjoyed throughout my playtime. Of course every game has its flaws but I think this does a good job of introducing new mechanics while still keeping the old Subnautica you like. All in all, the game is slightly different from the original but this doesn't take away from the experience, if you enjoyed the first Subnautica you'll enjoy this one too.

This game would've been alright if the main character just learned when to stop speaking, the writing in this game is terrible and extremely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ corny, and that would have been fine if the story wasn't the only thing the game had to offer. The game is extremely barebones in comparison to the first one, it only offers a few quality of life improvements, but at the same time it also ruins a few things that made the first game fun. An example I can give is the prawn suit, you cannot slingshot the prawn suit like the first game for movement speed, and I understand that the map is smaller but don't fix it if it ain't broke. There are also barely any deep biomes, the endgame area especially was so underwhelming. Don't even get me started with the abrupt ending. The map is smaller, and it wouldn't be an issue if they didn't cram so much ♥♥♥♥ together, it's way too dense, which leads to very abrupt biome changes, one minute you're in the floating lilypads biome, the next you're in the crystal caves. Also for a game about the ocean you spend a good chunk of the game exploring the arctic biomes on the surface, and this land biome has barely anything to offer, only one half baked leviathan and buggy snow stalkers.

Story 1/10
Music 9/10
Gameplay 8/10 (it's still subnautica, just a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ version of it so it can't be that much worse)

This game is another victim of being a spinoff title for a very good, loved and popular game. Everything they have done in this game was smaller in scale compared to the original Subnautica and I can see how that can deter players. 
The map is smaller in all three axes. 
Some features from the first game are not in this one, but they have added some new features that were not in the first game. 
The pacing of the game feels worse, but that could just be me. I got stuck in some places and was like “what now?”, because it was not so obvious that I needed to go deeper like it was in the first game. 
The story is interesting, though it is focused much more on telling a story directly, than on environmental storytelling like the first game was. 
I did enjoy the game a lot. Sure, it is a worse title than the first one, but it still is a good game worth playing, at least for the change of scenery and some features.

I really wanted to like this one. Everyone said that this game is pretty good if you don't listen to the haters. Unfortunately that's not true, and the problems really start to show quickly. Many questionable choices that leave you wondering if the development team really understood what made the first game worth playing. Studying alien life forms and seeing how they change from biome was interesting in Subnautica 1 but really feels lacking here. A lot of choices seem to be made for the sake of making the survival gameplay easier. Finding bladderfish all over the place when they were limited to the more shallow areas in the first game. A new plant that produces oxygen seemingly in every cave system makes diving to major checkpoints trivial. Even later you'll find rocks that continually produce oxygen with no lore behind why they are there and how they work, it seems like something the original wouldn't have glossed over. Navigating the world has been made more difficult via lack of distinct above sea level landmarks, very few undersea landmarks. Most of the areas you are supposed to visit are eventually revealed via waypoints and lit up with trails of lights. Biomes are seemingly smashed together with little respect to making a believable world as the first one did. Very few of the new creatures have interesting behaviors worth observing. I've experienced very poor performance on the steam deck despite it being verified and I've had several very nasty game breaking bugs. In one instance my prawn fell through the ground and floated forever downwards into nothingness until it was exploded by pressure losing several hours of gameplay. Speaking of gameplay, there are somethings I think were improved like the basebuilding, but you'll question your need to basebuild more than once in a world much smaller than the first game. I feel like I am slogging from checkpoint to checkpoint trying to soak up as much of the lore as I can. I'd say one of the biggest reasons to play this is to learn more lore about the subnautica universe. The new cutscenes are rough, human character models look like wacky borderlands rip offs. Lots of the new monsters just aren't that interesting in both sound and visuals, the leviathans just don't have much of a presence and don't feel like much of a threat. The sea truck is cool in theory, including its unique moonpool variant, but isn't much fun to use in practice. I found it getting stuck on things more often than I like, taking the train apart because it got jammed in a rock is tedious. Also another bug involving the sea truck that came up is that it would sometimes get stuck on nothing in the open ocean. Overall I'm actually not sure if I want to finish this, even just for the story which is a shame. I was a huge fan of the first game, played through it multiple times but I feel like the quality present there is severally lacking. I purchased this in a bundle with Subnautica 2, so it was heavily discounted. If you still want to experience Below Zero, I recommend you get it on a big discount.

honestly, its worth playing. for me, it took a few hours for me to actually start enjoying it instead of comparing it to the original.
24 hours to complete the story and the 2 optional achievements... mostly base building and exploring. 
sound track is great, atmosphere is good. 
the main downside is that there really isnt alot of handholding, i know its an exploration game, but there was points i was just lost, mainly becuase i lack any kind of critical thinking! (also it could be down to the PDAs i didnt read...)
 dont listen to the other reviews, youll learn to love the sea truck!

Overall, a very big downgrade from the original.

Sound design feels a lot louder compared to the first game, which makes it lose the feel the first game had. The Ice Worm and Snowfox are buggy beyond belief, that entire section of the game was a slog to get through. Story is kinda meh, its alright but some parts, particularly in Sam's storyline are incredibly underwhelming. 

Some parts I liked:

The art direction of the game is phenomenal, all the areas look really nice and are aesthetically pleasing. I like the seatruck as a vehicle, I do prefer the vehicles from Sub 1, but the seatruck works better for this game. Music is brilliant. The story with AL-AN is really fascinating and cool. Base building is better than the original. 

Not a bad game imo, its defo got reasons to play it. However the downsides are just a bit too big for me to ignore.

Below Zero is a good game with a beautiful world and interesting survival mechanics. However, the story feels pushed too aggressively — I wish the game gave more freedom instead of guiding the player along a very clear path.
It just never hit the same as subnautica 1 did. Recently playing subnautica 2 and it brings back that feeling of subnautica 1, more than this ever did.
Just get the first Subnautica its better in almost every aspect.
Not as great as the base game for me personally but still overal a fun and enjoyable game to pick up and play through if you enjoyed the other one.

Frustration simulator. In the early game upgrade fragments are everywhere. In the mid and late game most things can only be found one way. The game can't decide if it wants to be open ended or not which contributes to frustration. Some blueprints are open ended and can be discovered various ways. Other cannot. It's silly that I'm unlocking blueprints for cool stuff when my next task is to [spoiler] make a 1 hour trip to end the game. [/spoiler]

The first game was good about giving the player clear directions and technology for finding things. This game departs from that and uses clues and hints on some critical elements. It's frustrating because the player is trained by the game to play and progress a certain way, then the means become finding and listening to the right verbal/visual clues, brute force, or looking up the missing things on the internet.

The first 5-10 hours of this game is solid. The rest feels unfinished and unpolished.

I had a few issues with Below Zero, but overall I still think it's a solid game and thoroughly enjoyed my time with it. The base-building remains as engaging as ever. I spent countless hours expanding my base and getting completely sidetracked in the process. The new creatures are creative and memorable, with additions like the Sea Monkeys, Snow Stalkers and Penglings. And while this may be an unpopular opinion, I actually preferred the Seatruck over the Seamoth. I really liked the modular design and the freedom to customize it with different compartments to suit your playstyle.

That said, I do have some criticisms. The map feels noticeably smaller than the original, there are fewer leviathans and biomes to discover, and the story never quite captured my attention the way the first game's narrative did. I understand that Below Zero started out as more of an expansion than a full-fledged sequel, but it's hard not to compare it to the original experience.

Even so, I'd still recommend it to any fan of the first game. Its strengths far outweigh its shortcomings, and there's still plenty here to enjoy.

[spoiler] And while the Shadow Leviathans are undeniably terrifying at first, they eventually became more of an annoyance than a threat while exploring the Crystal Caves. They seem to aggro constantly, even when you're trying to stay out of sight. I also wish Marguerit had played a larger role in the story. Her survival and connection to the Degasi crew was one of the most fascinating mysteries from the first game, so I was hoping to see much more of her than what ultimately amounted to a fairly minor presence.[/spoiler]

Crap DLC to a GOAT Subnautica 1, hard to find out what to do, story sucks, all the crap about your sister doesn't matter, above ground movement is really bad, any minor increase in level stops you and you have to jump, which you can hardly do, only very small jumps, as good as it is under water, werid it is double as bad out of the water. I hope Subnautica 2 do not have this issue, not any above ground stuff :) 

The bad story made me stop playing this DLC oridginally, just finished it now a few days before Subnautica 2 releases for completeness, but still just like the original, a bug forced me to use console to complete and no achivement for leaving planet even if I left it in the end...

I know a lot of people dislike this one more than the first game but i love this one. A personal story and more information on the architects it is all just excellent for me. Honestly the underwater thing isn't what draws me to these games so i would have loved to continue where this game leaves off but that probably isn't going to happen. xP
 Honestly the best thing about this series is that it is a survival game with a clear story path.
Decent game, original Subnautica was significantly better. Hopefully Subnautica 2 is better.
Feels unfinished. Much smaller map, you explore it all within your first few hours. Story is just okay.

A smaller, more claustrophobic map leads to some frustratingly maze-like segments of the game that I really, really disliked. In the original subnautica, you only got lost because of mistakes of your own doing, in this game the level design is purposefully designed to keep you lost. Most of the time your target location is squirrelled away in small holes you've passed multiple times too.
The annoying main character, her quirky alien sidekick, and her quippy PDA don't help matters. Their stories are not particularly interesting nor impactful in a game like this and often disrupt your immersion, though nowadays they give you the option of almost entirely ignoring dialogues which is nice.
Really, if you really really like subnautica and want more of that kind of game loop, this is more than fine enough to entertain you. I wouldn't go so far as to recommend this game, however. It really is often a frustrating, irritating experience that I did not enjoy.

Sea truck is cool, I enjoyed that.

More Subnautica. Differs in terms of scale. Very vertically stacked and not as spacious. Claustrophobic tunnels with multiple layers for a biome. If the grand scale of Subnautica is what you're looking for, this ain't it. If you're looking for more aquatic survival or just more subnautica in general, it's a good choice. The storyline isn't crazy or anything super special, but it does expand the potential of the series. I'm excited for the next game so I decided to try and finish this one as fast as I could. I'm roughly a third of the way done at around 10 hours.

I'm not sure the developers completely understood what made the first Subnautica so successful when making this. Your character in the first game was mute - he assumed whatever narrative you wanted. In this game you play as an arrogant, sarcastic black lady that narrates everything. The poor writing starts right at the intro with "That did NOT go as planned! I should find a way into the water and get to the Drop Pod" - with a marker for the drop pod to go to.

The gameplay, especially early on, is largely similar to the first minus the narration. You swim around, collect resources, and get better gear - it's perfectly enjoyable at first. One of the main issues with the gameplay is that the world doesn't evoke thalassophobia like the first did, it feels much more claustrophobic with tight caves. The map is worse overall and much smaller.

Perhaps to compensate for the map being smaller, your movement speed and vehicle's speeds were made noticeably slower to the point where it's not as fun. The vehicles aren't great - there is no more Seamoth, and no larger vehicle like the Cyclops. Instead they opted to try to create a mobile base of sorts with the Seatruck, a modular vehicle that makes creating a base unnecessary. The Seatruck doesn't pair well with the tight corridors of the new map.

There are land sections that you can traverse on foot or with the new Snowfox vehicle. In the first game, land was a rarity that was a surprise to come by. I didn't feel that making land more common added anything positive to the game. Overall, I'd say my feelings are pretty bad about this game - play the original instead, or the new sequel where they returned to form.
Bite sized version of Subnautica. Got stuck late game and needed a few guides to get the rest of the way. Otherwise fantastic game, definately recommend.
3rd best game ever, second only to the original game and the new one

[h2]Warning! You will definitely die in this game at full health, with a full inventory and a gorgeous half-built base, killed not by a monster but by your own inability to remember that you need oxygen to live. But hey, at least the view was lovely.[/h2]

How much did I enjoy this game? Answer: Yes, with permanent thalassophobia!

Subnautica: Below Zero isn't just a survival game; it's a 30-hour negotiation between your sense of wonder and your sense of self-preservation. The ocean here is gorgeous in a way that feels almost cruel — you'll find a glowing reef bathed in bioluminescent green, take a moment to admire the way the light dances through the kelp, and then realize you've been admiring forty seconds too long.
I've been playing Below Zero exclusively in permadeath mode, and while I'd never say it's the only way to play, it definitely adds more immersion for the ones with the stomach for it. Permadeath turns every dive into a calculation — you don't go for a swim, you plan a route, count your oxygen, and accept that the ocean owes you nothing. The cost of a single mistake isn't a respawn screen; it's watching your entire save go dark in real time, fully aware, fully helpless, and completely out of options.
The leviathans come in two kinds. Some are genuinely peaceful and beautiful, drifting through the deep like the universe wants you to feel small in a nice way. Others exist to remind you that the universe also has teeth. But here's the thing: as primal as that fear is, there's a quieter, far more desperate way to die in this game.
O2 doesn't fight you. It just arrives, politely, like a silent assassin who booked the appointment weeks ago. By the time the edges of your screen start going black, there's a special kind of helplessness in swimming straight up, as hard as you possibly can, while some part of you already knows the surface is too far and the tank is empty.

Honest gripe: The Sea Truck is no comparison to he original's beloved Cyclops, it is not as cool, it doesn't have as many features, but we do what we can to make it feel like home.

Pro tip: Build an Air Bladder. Carry it everywhere. The difference between drowning and shooting to the surface like a panicked balloon is about two crafting components, and on a bad day it's the only thing between you and the loading screen.

I don't know what to say about this game. It feels like a mini version of the first game. The story is very dense and short, main characters motivations seem lacking and the whole world just feels off. I like the dialogue, even though it seems many don't like it. But it's not horrible and if you have that same itch as me to play Subnautica but with something new, this fixed it for me :D

Below Zero is definitely a weaker game than its predecessor. It’s not a bad game, and I actually enjoyed it quite a lot, but it removed much of what made the first Subnautica so great, which was the sense of mystery. In this game, you’re never really afraid to go into the depths because there’s barely any danger, and the leviathans aren’t nearly as creepy. It feels more like a peaceful underwater world, which hurts the atmosphere quite a bit. The story is now too linear and didn’t interest me that much, especially the storyline involving Sam. I’m also not a big fan of how much land there is now, because it wasn’t nearly as interesting as being underwater. However, it’s cool that they added the Snowfox as a new vehicle for land travel. It’s quite interesting how the game changed certain mechanics, like replacing the Cyclops with something that’s basically a combination of the Cyclops and the Seamoth: the Seatruck. Personally, I didn’t like it very much because of how easily it gets stuck and how slow it feels even with maximum upgrades. I do like the idea of the additional modules though, since they add a more tactical element to traveling, and it’s no longer as overpowered as the Cyclops was, which eventually made returning to your base unnecessary. You could say this game had a lot of interesting ideas that just didn’t fully work for me personally, and I still prefer the first game.

Gameplay - 9/10
Story - 7/10
Graphics - 10/10
Pacing - 7/10
Optimalization - 9/10
Soundtrack - 8/10
Audio - 10/10
I like this game. No where knew as good as the original but its still great. If this game came first people would praise it. 8/10

TLDR: Buy on sale.
While I do understand a lot of the communities gripes about this game I think it was really just a sidestep for the dev team to explore ideas they may have had and didn't want to implement in the first game. Nothing about it is downright bad, and none of the ideas are poorly executed or anything. I'm glad they never gave this game the name "2" because it absolutely would have never lived up to it.

The game has great new biomes and fauna, explores the concept of land in an ocean based game further than the original (although the clipping and movement can still be... downright bad at times.) and has a bunch of little gadgets that are definitely good additions, although carrying them all would leave you strapped for inventory room. My only complaint would be the majority of the collectible magnetite being gated by the spy pengling (more taken up inventory room) and spawning nowhere else but on land.

While the Chelicerae are personally a flop compared to the Reaper, the Shadow Leviathan is great.

For anyone who's completed the first game, this game is a breeze, and at full price I would absolutely not recommend it. It's a little over $1 an hour in gameplay if you only plan on playing thru it once.
The map does feel small but overall it's still a good game. Cant wait to play subnautica 2

This is a sandbox with a disjointed story. I actually accidentally played this thinking it was the original Subnautica and couldn't get the hype.

I love the mechanics in general but the land sections and totally broken story pacing are just too much to overlook. I can't wait to finish it and start the actual first game instead.

Just a long stretch of "This just aint it". Its not rlly bad, its just pointless. Adds nothing to the formular, removes some and didnt manage to rlly sell the new idea of the freezing cold. They managed to make it exciting for 2 and a half minutes to make a trailer and the rest ist jus bleh. It does have its moments but the progression is nowhere near as satisfying and exploration and the story lacks the IPs usual mystery. Good for a single playthrough but at that pricepoint just not worth it.

I'd like to comment on the story (spoiler-free) as may have already commented on the differences in gameplay between game entries:

>The story delves in to the universes lore in ways that just feel unnecessary, it's like someone took poly-filler to the the previous game's plot and called it a day. The game leaves no mystery or interest of it's own.

>I felt less immersed and invested in the characters with this entry, the voice-logs left behind by previous staff are numerous.. Many of them are interpersonal characterisations of the cast, which just come off  zany most of the time. It didn't feel like there was really any payoff for investing time to listen...
(compared to the first game's style of story telling, the concurrent nature of the survivors logs leaves you questioning if you really are alone, which encouraged my investment and added stakes to the story, which I really vibed with)

>The game takes an approach to story telling where the main character take a role as a semi-investigator, looking in to the actions of the previous research team on the planet. I actually like this approach, however the game underdelivers on this role, the main characters commentary on event's is pretty intrusive and often don't leave you with any questions or problems to solve for yourself.

>The game's tone feels like its changed for more of an adventure game with survival elements as opposed to the previous entry which I'd describe as a survival game at the core.

>I feel a lot less connected with the player character, she begins the game by throwing herself in to a survival situation with little care for risk, and she appears confident no matter what occurred during the plot, it's like she's written to be an action hero.. everything that happens in the plot Robin reacts to it like it's just another Tuesday, I could go in to detail here but I'd like to keep the review spoiler free for anyone considering the game. Ultimately I never felt like I could connect with Robin because she doesn't appear to experience any true hardship or dilemma? she doesn't feel very human to me in the end at least.
As an aside for characterisation, she does have a lot to say about her philosophy in the game, (when in discussion with the supporting cast) however I didn't find this interesting enough on it's own to really connect with her either.

>Small note on gameplay, many of the games areas feel like rabbit warrens, or a maze. There's no (or very few) meaningful landmarks to guide your way in the world, this approach left me feeling frustrated more often than not, it's as though the game was gating progression through obfuscation. I'd prefer a more dungeon-like approach, where areas are structured in a way that they can be explored more systematically. I'm happy with the occasional maze, but here it definitely felt overused.

I'm really left with no strong feelings for the game after completing it: give it a skip or play the first game in the series if you haven't.

Last note:

>FYI: the low hours invested in game before review are due to playing the game with a co-op mod, which expedited the completion.

>Question for anyone who's played the game, what should be the A plot, with Robins sister, ends up feeling like it's the B plot? (Amirite?)

So, like many people I have fond memories of watching Subnautica Let's Plays back in the day and always quite liked that game, but having seen playthroughs beginning to end there wasn't much of a surprise in that game for me. This one, though? I realised that I didn't know anything about it, and decided to buy it on a whim.

Long story short, I've heard many times that there's complaints about this game, but I personally adored it. The narrative focus is a nice added touch, the new section of the world and all the creatures in it are wonderful, the couple of new features compared to the first game are cool, the game is exactly the right amount of scary, etc. The list goes on.

Below Zero is not the same as the first Subnautica game. It's more narratively driven and gives the player less power over the world, keep these things in mind when you go in. You do not get the Cyclops or the Seamoth, there's new vehicles that are designed to make traversal a little bit more of an ordeal, complete with its hazards.

My only two complaints are that I was disappointed that the land area did not receive more focus, leading to one of the game's big enemies being somewhat single-issue, which surprised me, as well as that the game could've used a little bit more guidance on how to find two of its areas which eluded me for far too long?

Besides that? If you love the idea of Subnautica and you keep in mind that this one is less of a free sandbox and comes with more of a narrative, I would recommend the game without a doubt, especially if it's on sale!

EDIT: And as of writing this section I finally fully completed everything I set out to do and finished the story completely, and I love it so much. The narrative is so worth it, just do yourself a favor and follow the ominous blinking signals near the beginning of the game before I did!

Needless to say: 8 / 10, great game. There's things I'd love to see expanded or polished more in future entries, but for what it is and its price tag, I do not regret my purchase whatsoever.
Played for 16 hours. Came close to the end. Logged back on to beat the game and all of my items, blueprints, and data logs have been deleted. It was fun while it lasted, I guess.
i dont get the hate on this game, even if its worse than og subnautica its still goated

I started playing this game only because Subnautica 2 was releasing/has released. I loved Subnautica, I love the series and the vibe of the game. 
However, Below Zero does not live up to this vibe. It feels small. Rushed. Story things seem overlooked and unfinished. There were plenty of times where I wasn't really sure where to go next, and the ending felt super quick and sudden. I also felt like there were some plotholes, especially if you wouldn't have played the first game (what the heck are all these cables and structures and random pillars!). There are great functions in Subnautica, that weren't in Below Zero. Nothing feels lethal. I don't have the need to go deeper. I don't have the need to go back to 'dangerous biomes' over and over again; I go there once and I'm done. 

Of course there were still some positives: the flora and fauna looked great, biomes were pretty and the music!! The ending did leave me wondering and longing for more story after that; I would love to know what happened after. So that at least was very well done. 

Overall I rate this game a 6/10. I had fun playing it, but there were some frustrations and the story wasn't fulfilling. At least the game is on a deep sale regularly now.

Definitely worse than the original. The core gameplay aspects aren't bad, as they're similar to the first Subnautica although, my complaints are these: It has lot's of obnoxious and corny dialogues, specially from Robin, and even worse in the ending scene. The map feels crammed and not as mysterious as the first one. And finally, any land section of the game is pretty bad and annoying icl. Nevertheless, it's alright as I said, and reuses lot's of Subnautica's actually good mechanics so 7.5/10 or a tiny bit more idk.

So I’m a bit late for this game. After playing Subnautica for around 80 hours over a few years, I finally decided to try Subnautica: Below Zero. At first it felt kinda boring, but after some time it became just as addictive as the original. I’ve played around 18 hours on my save so far.
Once you really get into it, the game becomes extremely fun. If you’re like me and enjoy 100% completing games, there’s a lot to do — collecting every useful plant, fish, and egg, building a megabase, and just fully exploring everything the game offers.
I understand why some people hate on it, because it feels more like a DLC to the original rather than a completely new game. But honestly, I still really enjoyed it.
I’d definitely recommend playing it, but you should absolutely play the original before you dip your feet into the icy water of Sector ZERO. 
Now it’s time to enjoy the development of Subnautica 2.

Good game. Not as good as subnautica 1, but I still recommend it.

the part I really do not enjoy at all is the large land area. I recommend for that part you simply find a guide and follow it, to complete it as quickly as possible. the rest is really solid

I would have preferred less story like in subnautica 1, and also your "end game" vehicle, the sea truck, cannot fit well in some caves which is annoying, so the caves should be a bit wider. But it is not a huge deal

recommended game

kinda meh, original was better. this is a worse sequel, imo. tho, i must say base building is actually improved on here
In my opinion some people might say that this isnt a good game but I really enjoy it I think its fun.

With Subnautica 2 around 20 minutes away from EA launch, It's about time I gave this game a review. I certainly enjoyed this game and by no means is it "bad". In fact I enjoyed my first playthrough and still got terrified in areas, enjoyed making bases in biomes and exploring. That being said... it doesn't hold up to the first Subnautica what so ever and by that I think we all mean that is wasn't the same vibe. The isolation feeling and dark tone Subnautica had was lack luster in Below Zero, the story was "tolerable" at best but the lore for sure is there and I thought the EA story from the release of EA was actually more better (But I did enjoy the backstory of the aliens) and finally the biomes was just overall a bit "eh"- I also wish we had more creatures that had size. I feel like we see one creature more than anything else.

But with all of that, it is still good and I think we can put that aside when we understand it's a spin off than a sequel to Subnautica. There is still the "woah" moments and "Panic" moments but it's once or twice personally. But it is different and so the experience is worth the time to have a small change from the first titles vibe. 7/10

I have played this game in it's entirety and to be honest, I don't understand why this game gets so much backlash. The environment and gameplay was well, the content was quite unique and above all, the storyline was absolutely fascinating.

I would definitely recommend this to other gamers, whoever they might be.
if you love space,water and exploration this is the game for you
It has nos spirit, it feels like dead art. If you like Subnautica for it's mysteries, progression and fear factor then avoid this game as it is not a worthy successor.

About 7-8 years ago, I first found out there was a game where you had to survive on an alien planet covered mostly by water. I remember thinking, “Oh, I’ve never seen anything like this before, especially not on this scale. I need to try it.” And that’s how my journey with Subnautica started.

My first time playing was on console, and honestly, I had no idea what I was doing. I didn’t know where to go, what to look for, or how anything worked. I just started exploring the nearby waters, trying to find some kind of path forward. At one point, I noticed these little pebbles on the ocean floor. At first, I thought they were just part of the environment, but then I realized they were hiding useful materials inside. That small discovery helped me progress further into the game, and it made me wonder what else Subnautica was hiding from me.

That curiosity became one of the biggest reasons I fell in love with the game. There was always something more to discover, something that wasn’t obvious at first. I started seeing other people play it too - friends, YouTubers, streamers - and I loved watching how differently they approached the same world. I would explore places like the Jellyshroom Cave or the Lost River myself, and then watch how other people reacted when they reached those locations. It made me feel like I was part of something bigger, part of a massive community built around this incredible underwater world.

Later, when Subnautica: Below Zero arrived in Early Access, I was so excited. I had the chance to witness a whole new game being built from the ground up in a universe I already loved so much. I bought it even though it was still in Early Access, because I trusted the developers. I knew they were capable of creating another game that could mean as much to me as the first one did. That kind of trust - trusting people simply because you loved what they created before - is something huge. It’s not easy to earn.

Subnautica is also a game I feel like I grew up with, and that makes it matter even more to me. That’s why I want the next game to be as good as it possibly can be. Now, with Subnautica 2, I really hope they deliver a game I can spend countless hours in again. I love this universe. I love how they handle the community and the direction of the game. I love how they craft the gameplay, the atmosphere, the sense of mystery, and the feeling of discovery. I genuinely love everything they do to make people’s days better, even if only for a moment.

So yeah, Subnautica is a big part of my life now. So big that, at this moment, I honestly don’t think about much else.

I’m just waiting to finally try Subnautica 2 on May 14.

It's okay if you boo me. 
I like Below Zero more than the base game.

Things feel more cohesive, the narrative is more engaging for me, there's less aimless exploring as I'm rewarded for it instead of just being trapped in a 'go deeper' treadmill, the tools feel more purposeful, I like the variety of life, crafting seems more fun, I LOVE MY SEA TRUCK. I'm surprised the sea truck gets hate, it's the best. So versatile without being unwieldy.

I don't relate to any of the complaints I've seen, but I put off playing it for so long because everyone hates Below Zero. It was on sale, I figured why not to get a fix as I'm waiting to play the second installment after it leaves Beta (currently at least), and I've just been so pleasantly surprised.

I liked the base game but it didn't click with me fully. Outside of the ..laser incident, nothing surprised or engaged me overall. It was an empty planet and it felt like it. This feels more like a wreck for me, remnants of life. I'm having a great time.

And here we have "Subnautica 1.5". What can I say about this game? It's beautiful in every aspect.

There are several new additions to the base-building system, and the jukebox is a very welcome feature after the first game. The new interactions are amazing as well (like swimming alongside the whales), and they can truly take your breath away.

Of course, there are still those evil leviathans that want to take a bite out of you, which I think is essential to keep the spirit of Subnautica alive.

I feel like everything this game brings to the table only adds more charm to a series I grew up following. I'm genuinely happy to have experienced this masterpiece.

Recommended!

As of making this review I have now completed the game twice, and re-playing it after it's full release has been pretty fun. The thing that stood out to me the most playing BZ was the story, and closing a chapter on the Architects from the first game. I did really enjoy following the story of the second game, even though it was a bit chaotic and could at times be out of order following it.

I am really looking forward to see what Subnautica 2 has to offer, and can't wait to see how the development will go moving forward.

Over all if you are a fan of the first game I would recommend giving BZ a try as well, even though I might not have enjoyed it as much as the first game it was still an enjoyable experience!

Subnautica: Below Zero takes a slightly different approach compared to the original game by focusing more on narrative and atmosphere while still maintaining the survival mechanics that make the series so engaging. The frozen environment feels hostile in a completely different way, with snowstorms, icy caves, and freezing temperatures adding constant pressure during exploration. This creates a more grounded and claustrophobic experience, especially when moving between underwater depths and dangerous land sections.

What makes the game stand out is how it mixes calm exploration with moments of uncertainty and tension. Discovering abandoned facilities, strange lifeforms, and hidden parts of the map always feels rewarding, especially because the game rarely interrupts the player with unnecessary guidance. The addition of new tools, creatures, and vehicles keeps progression satisfying throughout the adventure, while the smaller but denser world design makes every biome feel distinct and memorable.
Its nice and short. Pretty easy 100%, you just need to follow the story.

I liked it a lot. The biomes look amazing, they story is not a blockbuster hollywood movie but still enjoyable and fun.
AL-AN's humor is a 10/10.

If you like exploring (and a bit of base building), this is for you.

It is not a strong as its first one, thats for sure. I think it has a really decent mid-game, but early and late are an absolute pain. Most of the time you are searching for the next storyline to trigger and there arent many to begin with. Still the music, voice acting, atmosphere and the game itself (encountered no bugs etc.) are quite solid. I would recommend it on a discount.
I remember when the devs were gonna add this as an expansion or dlc to the first game so I'm still salty about that ♥♥♥♥. ♥♥♥♥ unknown worlds and their overlords.

This is a fun subnautica game, but definitely the weakest in the series. The writing for the characters story is complete ass. The dialogue was insufferable and cheesy. The sister is essentially a terrorist and the main character is thinks she's some scientist.  The character movement is slow compared to the other games and not much reason to make a base outside of starting area. The seatruck sucks and there isn't much to do. Felt more like a chore than a story.  Only buy if on sale and want to play all the subnautica games.

It feels like the game regressed in certain areas compared to Subnautica 1. There was definitely more story to be invested in, and that was the part I liked.
However, I had to ask for help or look up solutions many times. Even the Rebreather was left into a single databox that the player needs to stumble into. It's absolutely baffling to me how many things feel like a less polished version of the first game's mechanics.

The game is really good runs smoothly and story seems a bit disconnected from the original, makes sense why it was originally planned to be a DLC, the main vehicle [spoiler] seatruck [/spoiler] is slow, annoying and doesn`t use vertical space like the original mobile base, the dialogue seems like it came from a children`s novel, and you are much slower in general which does NOT help the map feel bigger, and leviathans felt like obstacles more then they were fear or curiosity inducing, beyond that it`s a good sequel that opens new potential for subnautica 2`s story, the gameplay is nearly identical to the original which is nice, overall 8/10 game, 6/10 as an expansion.
FMC is slow and the game feels more claustrophobic than thalassophobic. Subnautica 1 is better in every way especially story and presentation.

23.5 hours, decided to play this before subnautica 2, it's ok ima be real. Game felt slow despite being smaller than first game, smaller map, shallower map, leviathans not scary, was more scared fighting a peeper. If you want gameplay this could work i suppose.

Wow, not what I expected. Coming from replaying Subnautica 1 to this the game just isn't fun Yes it's basically the same game with horrible voice acting, bugs, and a lack of story that doesn't make any sense. The leviathans are cool, except the void leviathans they are just the same as the ones you can encounter without going to the void. The Seatruck sucks it's useless to go really deep with the vehicle mods the bike is cool but sometimes in my playthrough the snowworm launched me extremely high up kinda funny tho but shouldn't be possible and again the ending just doesn't make sence the first one made it clear throughout the story that the main character would have consequences when leaving the planet with the extreme debt he was accumulating. I loved the first game and followed it for years in early access even when I hated the fact it was in development but this definitely set a new low now that the new one is close to releasing again in early access im worried they'll deliver the same dumpster fire this was specially with the legal battle thing that happened or is happening with the devs keep your expectations mild but we shall see thx for reading.

Pretty aight, 7/10, suffers from millenial writing and disfunctional plot. but gameplay is pretty nice and graphics rock. the world feels a lot more closed in then subnautica 1 and feels more packed in general, the land sections were interesting but kinda eh
subnautica 1 is better, but this is still good, ish
Can't stand protagonist, has too much personality for this type of game for my taste. Silent character like in first game would be much better.

Can't recommend. There's a bunch that's fun to explore, but after the umpteenth time getting stuck out of bounds for taking a walk in an exploration game, it gets old. There's a debug feature that never helps, and a popup reminding you that you can complain, which also does not help. The game remains broken.

Return to planet 4546B to expand on the lore of the Architects and the planet itself. It mostly feels like Subnautica with the vagueness of it all. It also has more above-ground exploration (and a cool hoverbike). Overall a decent game, and hence recommended, but I am on the edge to be honest.

The story has three major "parts", two of which are implicitly linked, but you can complete them in any order, or without even figuring out the other, which can be confusing. However, my main gripe is when the plot twist gets revealed, the main character does not acknowledge it at all, even when it goes against what she knows of her own sister. At least not when I finished the parts in the order I did.

The ground areas are almost too large, and not that fun to explore either. Sure they are a nice twist and addition to the otherwise familiar systems, but still, a bit large in my opinion.

Admittedly, I also somewhat burned out during the last parts of the game and just looked up the final piece I was missing to complete the game, as well as the location of the Cold Suit for the achievement. Not too much to my surprise, both were in areas I already had been to, but due to the size of it all, had missed. The intrinsical Subnautica experience.

100% achievements in about 26 hours (looking up a thing or two). Skipping building a second base and unnecessary traveling between them could shave off a few hours. Finding everything on my own would probably add 5.
It's basically the first subnautica all over again, different story, different location, same mechanics. Less scary than the first time around. It's not *vastly* different to the first one.

It's more Subnatuica so if that's all you want then go for it, but it's not as good as the first one and a lot of the design decisions feel sloppy. It's messy and was thrown together to capitalise on the first one. The story feels more like an intrusion into the experience than something the player really feels compelled to explore, despite it being our sister we're looking for! It often feels like the player and the various other supporting voices fail to react to things that they should in order to help guide the player a bit, while at the same time the player gets hud icons for pretty much everything worth checking out but little understanding of what to do when you get there. Like I said, messy. In terms of survival and exploration: there are certainly some new things to see and new creatures to find but also various elements are missing that were in the original (no sub for instance, probably due to the condensed space) and sometimes things just don't make sense because of pasifistic dev sensitivities. 

I needed fur from a specific animal so I do the obvious thing and take down one of them with the knife I have. I'm not allowed to harvest the fur from the animal though, this is a "no weapons" game and violence on the part of the user is verboten in this highly hostile environment... very dumb, especially considering how amusing the noise is when you accidently hit small fish with the sea truck 

Why have I put so many hours in? Started several times, never bothered to finish it, kept forgetting why.

Had much fun to 100% the game. Like the first Subnautica it was worth every minute. 
Also it has a nice story again.
Not as good at the first game but still a good game, and with a lot of lore, could it be better ? yes is it still good ? yes

Easy going and starting on creative mode gives you the opportunity to get a feel for things, before hitting survival mode. The only downside I can see, and if I've missed it please let me know, there is no easy to read map. I'm not a lover of 1st person games, but had no problems with this game and motion sickness.
It's kinda like Subnautica but you have to listen to HR the entire time. I would skip it and play the 2nd one instead

the first game was good, solid gameplay, pretty clear on what im doing. These 2.6 hours have been pure and utter confusion, with no hints and constant dying while walking around the same 300m radius looking for the basic materials for the basic crafts, this feels like a rage game when its meant to be a horror game. just my personal experience, maybe i'll convince myself to beat it but i cant bring myself to play this at the moment. the seatruck sucks, just a worse seamoth. the main character also wont shut up no offense, ryley was way better. One positive is this game has good graphics and also I like the arctic area. looking forward to subnautica 2!
good game but still subnautica is better
Not nearly as good as the original, and those Sea Monkey's can get stuffed, there should be an option to remove those things from the game, they are just a massive pain in the arse.

100% achievement review
The game was a little behind compared to Subnautica. For me it only had better building details. Environment, story, exploration, leviathans... They were lacking. It had good scenery in a few places but it was just it. Still it could've been worse.
Would give 6.1/10

its fun but the map is kinda small i dont reccomend that much but the vehicles are nice like the seatruck subnautica 1 is better tho its good but dosent rlly have that much vehicles js a prawn suit and the seatruck if u like easy games its kinda good it has 5 modes survival freedom hardcore creative custom
oh yes, its a good game, but not actually as good as the original Subnautica, if the og was like a 10/10 this is about an 8/10, still pretty good.

I just can't bring myself to like it. Outside of QOL features it's just a downgrade to original.
Execute the mapper please

This game is inferior to the first one in many ways. If you thought the first game was sort of vague and even confusing in what you needed to do, this one is worse. 

Other crappy things to note: Your Prawn suit no longer keeps momentum when using the grappling arm

Leviathans can smell you a mile away, even if you're out of sight so expect to be attacked A LOT. Cant hide from these guys I guess.

Same awkward collision from the first game so expect to get hung up on a lot of stupid objects.

Your Seatruck (replaces the Cyclops) has very limited storage space and wont let you add more storage compartments (like the Cyclops will) which means you are always out of inventory space and constantly have to backtrack for crap you need later. You could add another storage module but its unnecessary bloat to an already crappy mechanic.

Last but certainly not least, this is a survival game. If something is trying to eat me, I should be allowed to kill it. Instead you dont have any good weapons to defend yourself. So if you hit something large with a shock or whatever, then a few seconds later its back to chewing on your ass again. All this because a dev wants to shove his bs morals in our faces. . .  

PS. When one of the smaller predators start biting me, I hit it with a sea truck. Shove it, devs!

This game is definitely fun. Resource collection is definitely different to Subnautica because each outcrop produces Titanium and its own specific resource, so you have to know what outcrop to look for. Resources also feel a bit more scarce despite the largely increased number of outcrops mainly because of what I previously mentioned. Definitely have to get used to the temperature meter, because it forces you to be in the water or inside shelter more often.

Great game. Honestly, I love the graphics, love the story, and enjoy the fact that it takes a different turn with ALAN, so the isolation aspect is still there but just not as strong... My laptop specs ran great on it (on high settings). The ending is spectacular but does leave you wanting to find out more, but that just signals to me that it was a great game... The only thing is that sometimes both Sam's story and ALAN's story are sometimes difficult to combat at once, finish Sam's story first is my recommendation.

So far I don't understant the critcs to this game... I been actually finding this game way darker and scarier than the first, ok I agree the squid-shrimp leviathan is not as scary as reaper, but the smaller monters are WAAAAAY scarier thant the one from the first, and the talassophobia is on point in this game man sheeesh! Loving every second of it, i'm still pretty early in the main story, but so far I like the new protagonist, recomend it to anyone who enjoyed the first game!

Fun relaxing game to winde to. I like the music and the story.
Both games are come back to play again and again.
Waiting to start Subnautica 2 already loaded and ready to play.
A big downgrade from the first game. Story is poorly thought out and ends up undermining the story from the previous game.

Not as good as the first one in terms of story and finding out where you need to go but still overall a good game. [spoiler]Of course only when you're under water. Everything that's on land in this game is just awful.[/spoiler]

This game differs from the base game in many ways. It made some controversial changes to the formula, and tried to fill in some of the many, many unanswered mysteries that the first game left unanswered.

The change in formula from a silent protagonist stranded alone with only a damaged PDA for company, to a chatty, bratty, yappy protagonist calling people or being called constantly is barely tolerable.

RIP [spoiler] Cyclops [/spoiler], you will be missed. The loss in storage space and freedom to build/decorate compared to its clunky, modular replacement is devastating.
Changing up some of the recipes and having to improvise was a fresh take at least.

Making certain materials almost inaccessible just to gate progression (Lithium and Magnetite), is absolutely awful. Removing all large resource deposits except for Copper, Titanium and Gold while gating storage is even worse.

The solitary adventure tone is also completely gone when friendly creatures start giving you resources.

In short, I was willing to forgive many things and maybe rate this a 6 or 7 to the original game's 9 or 10. But having my almost-endgame (just lacking Kyanite) Prawn 1-shot thanks to an unpatched bug [spoiler]a frost worm spawning under it[/spoiler],  and take most of my endgame materials with it, is not forgivable.

The game's been out a long time, not patching that by now is unforgivable. So is disabling achievements if you use the console to get back materials lost thanks to bugs like this (and having them clip through the floor). That also happened on the base game, but nothing on this level of ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.

If you want to know what happens, just watch the cutscenes on YT, or read the wiki. If you decide to play, save before entering a dangerous area and quit without saving if your high-investment equipment gets deleted. I'm not going back to 4 hours since my last save.
Worst of the Subnautica series. Less of everything, with almost the same amount of content as in Subnautica 2 which just started early access.

Honestly i do enjoy subnautica below zero but i cannot in good faith recommend it to anyone, ESPECIALLY not people new to the subnautica franchise. If you want a good game, go play subnautica 1, it is infinitely better. This game is just so slow, cramped, and annoying to play

How is there NO AUTOSAVE in a game released in 2021? That is SHAMEFUL. Not so much as a warning to the player. I just lost my first 1.5 hours of progress because of a crash. Something that can most definitely be implemented with a simple save running on a timer and they didn't bother to add it. These developers deserve every single downvote they get for not adding such a simple, standard feature to a game with so much grind.

Edit: I'm not even going to bother playing this one. It'll sit in my library simply because I got it for $4 alongside Subnautica 2. If I didn't have to refund both games at once then re-buy Subnautica 2 (due to them being part of the same sale), then I'd take my $4 back instead of having this lying around unplayed.
Played this game for 100+ hours on Xbox. Many say this is was way worse than the first game, but I love it just as much as the first one. In my top 3 favorite games of all time.
I'm not much of a gamer, but this is a game I've put hours into. I would recommend this game to anyone with s Steam account.
could have a couple improvements to movement and some other things, but all around a beautiful game with lots of fun things to explore
I really enjoy the continued look into the previous world. Getting to see the cold water biomes is awesome.
I dont think its as good as the first game but it was still fun to play. Would recommend
Subnautica BZ is both underhated, and underrated, It's more akin to a DLC expansion, and when viewed from that perspective, It easily holds itself as a light 8/10

There's a lot I didn't like about the game, but ultimately it wasn't bad. I wouldn't pay full price, but for $8 on sale, I feel like I got my money's worth. I appreciated the additional decorations, posters and things I could add to my base. Made it feel more homey.

It's a fun game, that's not too constrained. Resources are finite, so some planning may be needed depeding on how creative you want to be.

Story is directive on where you need to go, bu there is enough additional content that gives the story more depth.

Its a fun game, though it also feels less captivating then the first game. The story itself is good and the environments are beautifully done, but the vehicles are worse than the first game, and the land portion of it really left a sour taste with just how meh it is, as well as how jank the snowfox felt overall.

Overall, 7/10
this game is underrated the story is good the map is good and the ending is good
i thought it was a good subnautica continuation, the only part i dont like is sometimes you feel stuck unlike subnautica 1, but is still really good

An excellent sequel to the original game, which I also LOVED. It keeps the intriguing and genuinely awesome esence of the first Subnautica and adds many more new features and lore and concepts. 10 thumbs up out of 1.
This game is stunning and has, in my opinion, a better story with an incredible ending. This game is severely over-hated and I would personally give it a 10/10.
